016package org.consensusj.jsonrpc;
017
018import java.io.IOException;
019import java.util.Optional;
020import java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture;
021import java.util.concurrent.Executor;
022import java.util.function.BiFunction;
023import java.util.function.Consumer;
024import java.util.function.Predicate;
025import java.util.function.Supplier;
026
027/**
028 * Helper methods for creating asynchronous calls using {@link CompletableFuture}. Since the
029 * synchronous methods in {@link DefaultRpcClient} throw checked exceptions this interface
030 * provides wrapper support to make it easier to convert them to async calls.
031 */
032public interface AsyncSupport {
033    /**
034     * Supply async for a ThrowingSupplier by catching exceptions and completing exceptionally.
035     *
036     * @param throwingSupplier Supplier of T that can throw an {@code IOException}
037     * @param <T> return type
038     * @return A completable future, returning T
039     */
040    default <T> CompletableFuture<T> supplyAsync(ThrowingSupplier<T> throwingSupplier) {
041        return supplyAsync(throwingSupplier, getDefaultAsyncExecutor());
042    }
043
044    /**
045     * Supply async for a ThrowingSupplier by catching exceptions and completing exceptionally.
046     *
047     * @param throwingSupplier Supplier of T that can throw an {@code IOException}
048     * @param executor Executor to run the Supplier
049     * @param <T> return type
050     * @return A completable future, returning T
051     */
052    default <T> CompletableFuture<T> supplyAsync(ThrowingSupplier<T> throwingSupplier, Executor executor) {
053        return AsyncSupport.supplyCatchingAsync(throwingSupplier, executor);
054    }
055
056    /**
057     * Return the default executor for supplying asynchronicity.
058     *
059     * @return An executor.
060     */
061    default Executor getDefaultAsyncExecutor() {
062        return (Runnable r) -> new Thread(r).start();
063    }
064
065    private static <T> CompletableFuture<T> supplyCatchingAsync(ThrowingSupplier<T> throwingSupplier, Executor executor) {
066        CompletableFuture<T> future = new CompletableFuture<>();
067        executor.execute(() -> {
068            try {
069                T result = throwingSupplier.getThrows();
070                future.complete(result);
071            } catch (IOException e) {
072                future.completeExceptionally(e);
073            }
074        });
075        return future;
076    }
077
078    /**
079     * Subinterface of {@link Supplier} for Lambdas which throw {@link IOException}.
080     * Can be used for two purposes:
081     * <ol>
082     *     <li>To cast a lambda that throws an {@code IOException} to a {@link Supplier} while
083     *      automatically wrapping any exception thrown by the lambda with {@link RuntimeException}.</li>
084     *     <li>As a {@code FunctionalInterface} where a lambda that throws exceptions is
085     *      expected or allowed.</li>
086     * </ol>
087     * This is intended to be used to wrap JSON-RPC I/O methods of {@link JsonRpcClient} that throw {@link IOException} and
088     * subclasses such as {@link JsonRpcException}, so we have narrowed the allowed exceptions in {@link #getThrows()} to
089     * {@link IOException}.
090     *
091     * @param <T> the type of results supplied by this supplier
092     */
093    @FunctionalInterface
094    interface ThrowingSupplier<T> extends Supplier<T> {
095
096        /**
097         * Gets a result wrapping checked Exceptions with {@link RuntimeException}
098         * @return a result
099         */
100        @Override
101        default T get() {
102            try {
103                return getThrows();
104            } catch (final Exception e) {
105                throw new RuntimeException(e);
106            }
107        }
108
109        /**
110         * Gets a result.
111         *
112         * @return a result
113         * @throws IOException A (checked) exception
114         */
115        T getThrows() throws IOException;
116    }
117
118    /**
119     * Error filter for resilient polling. Uses a Predicate to specify what to ignore and a Consumer to log
120     * what is ignored.
121     */
122    interface TransientErrorFilter {
123        static TransientErrorFilter of(Predicate<Throwable> filter, Consumer<Throwable> logger) {
124            return new TransientErrorFilter() {
125                @Override
126                public boolean isTransient(Throwable t) {
127                    return filter.test(t);
128                }
129
130                @Override
131                public void log(Throwable t) {
132                    logger.accept(t);
133                }
134            };
135        }
136
137        static TransientErrorFilter none() {
138            return of(
139                    (t) -> false,   // No errors are consider transient
140                    (t) -> {}       // Nothing to log because we're not swallowing anything.
141            );
142        }
143
144        /**
145         * Handler to transpose to a "future maybe". Use with {@link CompletableFuture#handle(BiFunction)}
146         * followed by {@code .thenCompose(Function.identity())} (or if JDK 12+ {@code CompletableFuture#exceptionallyCompose(Function)})
147         * to swallow transient errors.
148         * @param result T
149         * @param t An error, possibly transient
150         * @return A completable future of future maybe
151         * @param <T> The desired return type
152         */
153        default <T> CompletableFuture<Optional<T>> handle(T result, Throwable t) {
154            if (result != null) {
155                return CompletableFuture.completedFuture(Optional.of(result));
156            } else if (isTransient(t)) {
157                log(t);
158                return CompletableFuture.completedFuture(Optional.empty());
159            } else {
160                return CompletableFuture.failedFuture(t);
161            }
162        }
163
164        // TODO: Should this be flipped to isFatal()?
165        boolean isTransient(Throwable t);
166        void log(Throwable t);
167
168    }
169}
